title: Partition work-tracking: backlog, current sprint, archived sprints
problem_statement: A single issues.json carries every issue ever filed — 23 after one sprint, more the moment the in-flight catalog files — and grows without bound. Agents that need only the open backlog or only the active sprint must load and parse the whole history, and the file becomes a merge hotspot since every state change for any issue touches one document.
solution: Partition issues by lifecycle location, per Brooks's rulings: .excn/issues/backlog.json holds open unpulled issues; each sprint's pulled issues live in .excn/issues/sprint-N/sprint-N-issues.json, which becomes that sprint's archive by construction when the sprint closes. Sprint records stay at .excn/sprints/. An issue's location IS its state: filed → backlog, pulled → the sprint's issues file, closed → stays there. scribe moves issues at sprint boundaries. Every file stays small and conforms to the same issue schema.

user_stories.want: a backlog file containing only open, unpulled issues
user_stories.benefit: I load candidates without parsing history

user_stories.want: the active sprint's pulled issues in one small sprint-issues file
user_stories.benefit: the in-flight issue set is one read, separate from history and backlog

user_stories.want: issue movement to be file movement (backlog → sprint → archive)
user_stories.benefit: location encodes state and the long-file merge hotspot disappears

user_stories.want: each past sprint's issues archived in their own file beside its sprint record
user_stories.benefit: a sprint's history is two small named files, not rows buried in an unbounded log

user_stories.want: sprint-completion checks reading the sprint record plus its sprint-issues file
user_stories.benefit: my rubric reads two small documents instead of the full issue history

user_stories.want: a stable publish target for new issues
user_stories.benefit: publishing appends to the backlog regardless of sprint state

user_stories.want: the partitioned layout stamped by the template with matching schemas
user_stories.benefit: new Instances start partitioned instead of migrating later

implementation_decisions.title: Partition topology (ruled)
implementation_decisions.summary: .excn/issues/backlog.json holds open unpulled issues; .excn/issues/sprint-N/sprint-N-issues.json holds each sprint's pulled issues — the active sprint's file is simply the latest N whose sprint record is active; past files are the archive by construction. Sprint records stay at .excn/sprints/. No special current-vs-archive file shapes.

implementation_decisions.title: One schema everywhere (ruled)
implementation_decisions.summary: An issue is an issue — the data does not change with location. Every partition file conforms to the same issue collection schema; assigned_sprint and closed_in_sprint survive as record fields (location organizes, fields record). No backlog- or archive-specific schemas.

implementation_decisions.title: scribe moves files at sprint boundaries (ruled)
implementation_decisions.summary: At sprint start scribe moves pulled issues backlog.json → sprint-N-issues.json; at sprint close, issues that didn't resolve return to backlog.json and closed issues stay in the sprint's file as its archive.

implementation_decisions.title: Cross-file invariants
implementation_decisions.summary: Issue ids stay globally unique across all partitions; depends_on may reference issues in any partition; validation (scribe's field-by-field checks, the schemas) must check across files, not within one.

implementation_decisions.title: Schema changes ride the partition
implementation_decisions.summary: issue.schema and sprint.schema evolve to the partitioned shape; the template stamps the partition skeleton; every path/contract reference (scribe definition, process-adherence rubric, PROCESS.md, to-issues publish target) updates in the same change window.

implementation_decisions.title: Live migration
implementation_decisions.summary: The root Instance's issues.json and sprint_1.json split into the new partitions with zero data loss — scribe owns the state migration, Team Lead owns template/doc changes, Src-First order throughout.

implementation_decisions.title: Ships with the 0.3.x line
implementation_decisions.summary: Lands alongside or immediately after the Work Grill fork (EXEC-007 set) to avoid two consecutive stamped-layout migrations for consumers.

testing_decisions: Migration acceptance: every issue id present exactly once across partitions post-split; counts reconcile (filed = backlog + current + archived); all cross-file depends_on resolve.
package-qa gates the template/CLI ride-along; registry acceptance stays the npx-vs-local byte-diff pattern.
scribe's validators extend to cross-file checks and run after every partition move.
out_of_scope: The Work Grill fork itself (EXEC-007 set) — separate PRD-001 lineage.
Retros/PRDs/progress files — partition covers issues and sprints only; the rest of the work-tracking set is untouched.
Issue-tracker integrations beyond the file layout.
notes: Shape stated by Brooks 2026-06-07; detail rulings landed 2026-06-08 in the breakdown review: uniform schema ('an issue is an issue, the data in the issues doesn't change, just the location'), topology issues/backlog.json + issues/sprint-#/sprint-#-issues.json, scribe owns the boundary moves. The planned design-grill slice was dropped — nothing material remained open.
Sprint-2 candidate set; filed during sprint 1's close ceremony.
